existing_Entity_Description: Sunshine Coast Regional District is a regional district in Gulf Islands & Sunshine Coast, British Columbia, Canada, within the Sunshine Coast. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, Sunshine Coast Regional District had a population of 33,000. The community is situated in idyllic island and coastal communities accessible only by ferry or boat. ferry-dependent communities with strong local loyalty and seasonal tourism. The region features mild Mediterranean-like microclimate — the warmest in Canada.

• Regional District: Sunshine Coast
• Type: Regional District
• Climate: mild Mediterranean-like microclimate — the warmest in Canada
• Character: artists, retirees, and nature lovers drawn to island tranquility
• Distance from Vancouver: ~55 km

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Rain-shadow mildness on the southern Gulf Islands (among Canada's sunniest coastal spots); the Sunshine Coast lives up to its name with drier summers than Vancouver but still wet winters; everything is marine-exposed.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: An archipelago of forested islands in the Salish Sea and a mainland coast strip (the Sunshine Coast) reachable only by ferry or air despite being on the mainland; rocky shorelines, ferry-dependent logistics.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: Tourism, arts communities, retirees, small-scale agriculture and aquaculture; ferry schedules shape all commerce; Salt Spring Island's Saturday Market and Powell River's converted mill economy are regional anchors.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Cottages and character homes, high-value waterfront properties, off-grid and rural systems (wells, septic), and older housing needing marine-climate upkeep; island logistics raise every project's complexity.
